denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 03/15/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W101354149          Group #: GRP-1171
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-127448
Claim #: CLM-2026-197064
Date of Service (requested): 02/23/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Summit Specialty Associates

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination regarding prior authorization. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 02/25/2026 for Duopa pump evaluation related to Parkinson disease, motor fluctuations (G20). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Duopa pump evaluation
Diagnosis (as submitted): Parkinson disease, motor fluctuations (G20)
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service.
Clinical policy applied: CPB 0111 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
This request has been processed under a benefit category that does not provide coverage for the requested service (benefit category classification).
Our review applied the criteria in CPB 0111 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Duopa pump evaluation is non-preferred; a formulary alternative must be tried for Parkinson disease, motor fluctuations (G20). A lower-tier agent is available without demonstrated failure of the preferred option.

The file does not satisfy utilization-management requirements for formulary tier dispute. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with an Aetna medical director to review clinical information before you file a written appeal., they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W101354149) and reference PA-2026-127448. 

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department

This determination was made by Dr. J. Smith, Medical Director.

This determination was made by Dr. J. Smith, Medical Director.
clinical_context: The 49-year-old member has Parkinson disease, motor fluctuations (G20). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Duopa pump evaluation recommended as the next appropriate step. Off-time 6 hours daily despite optimized oral levodopa. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that formulary tier dispute bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Aetna correspondence with CPB and InterQual fingerprints and procedural tone.
